bullets.text: Ukrainian President Zelensky's decision to remove a popular defense minister has sparked protests in Ukraine, reportedly due to a rift between the minister and the Commander-in-Chief.
Iran reportedly targeted military bases overnight, with explosions heard across the country, shortly after neighboring Gulf states reported attacks, while the US launched a wave of strikes.
A teenager has been accused by Polish security services of carrying out sabotage actions in Poland for Russia, with the aim of inciting ethnic tensions between Poland and Ukraine.
The Australian Taxation Office is facing scrutiny over its decision to spend at least $73.4 million to move its Adelaide offices a short distance to a new building.
New South Wales health authorities have reported a significant decline in infant hospitalisations for a serious respiratory virus, attributing the success to a vaccination program.

Australia is bracing for a potential Super El Ni

o, with the Bureau of Meteorology forecasting an increased chance of unusually warm and dry spring conditions for most capital cities, and climatologists warning 2027 could be the hottest year on record.
Russian strikes are disrupting Ukraine's Black Sea grain trade, causing wheat prices to surge due to heavy attacks.
Emerging market currencies have fallen as increased tensions between the US and Iran strengthen the dollar, with the US launching new strikes.
A selloff in chipmakers is dragging down stocks amid concerns about whether massive artificial intelligence investments will justify their high valuations, alongside geopolitical risks.
